title: WIP: Cirrus: Integration-test sub-sets
body:
As tests are added, total runtime increases beyond (IMHO) optomal 45
minutes (more or less) for PR testing.  Fix this by utilizing an
additional matrix of VMs to divide up testing for parallel execution.

fixes #2649

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:

We suggest the following additional approver: **mheon** If they are not already assigned, you can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @mheon` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

***NOTE:*** This is not fully-cooked, beware of consuming raw meat.

The `1_3`, `2_3`, and `3_3` files are not ready yet, and I'm not even sure my scripting is sane :grin:

@baude WDYT about this approach (selectively deleting test source files)?

TBH, I kinda don't like the explosion of new tests, and it complicates debugging: "find `$NEEDLE` results" among so many `$HAYSTACKS`
